observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require
the GASS Office and Accounting Office to
expedite the implementation of the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed
under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to finally address the
perennial issue of the unreliability of PPE
balances presented in the financial
statements.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
(3) a) Direct the personnel concerned to closely
monitor the Municipality’s expenditures
to avoid cash insufficiency; and
(4) b) Require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that monies held in trust be used
only for the intended purpose; and
(5) c) Refrain from disbursing funds for
expenditures without the corresponding
appropriation therefor, and incurring
obligations when no cash is available to
cover the same.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant complied with the audit recommendations.
Austerity measures were instituted to lessen the incurrence of overdraft.
observations.reason: Disbursements were not properly managed due to a lack of funds, which resulted from not meeting target revenues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
(6) a) Require the concerned officers and
employees to settle and/or liquidate their
cash advances and strictly adhere to the
rules and regulations on the grant,
utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances;
(7) b) Cause the withholding of salaries of
accountable officers who are still in Deductions were made
government service or facilitate effecting from the salary of the
the same, until the unliquidated cash employees with
advances were settled; unliquidated cash
(8) c) Observe compliance with the provisions advances.
of COA Circular 2012-004, particularly
in relation to the accountable officers
already separated from government
service and whose unliquidated cash
advances remained unsettled as of
December 31, 2011; and
(9) d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the
previous cash advance of the Disbursing
Officer is settled first before a grant of
additional cash advance is allowed.
observations.managementAction: The recommended action is partially implemented. Deductions were made from the salary of the employees with unliquidated cash advance, who issued written authority to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason:
Some employees are
non-responsive to the
demand letter issued by
the Municipal
Administrator.
The recommended action
is partially implemented.
Deductions were made
from the salary of the
employees with
unliquidated cash
advance, who issued
written authority to the
Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
(10) a) Ensure that a Memorandum of
Agreement be secured first as required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001, first
before undertaking projects involving
inter-agency fund transfers;
(11) b) Instruct the personnel concerned to
conduct periodic analysis, review, and
monitoring of all fund transfers to ensure
compliance with all requirements stipulated
in the MOA as well as in other relevant laws
and regulations;
(12) c) Require the Municipal Accountant to
individually verify and validate the
Subsidiary Ledger account balances relating
to various fund transfers, especially prior
years’ balances, to establish obligations
therefrom and effect the necessary
adjusting journal entries, if warranted; and
(13) d) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return
any unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned,
as stipulated in the MOA.
observations.managementAction:
Memorandum of Agreement is secured first before undertaking projects involving inter-agency fund transfers.
Responsible personnel were directed to perform regular analysis, review, and monitoring of all fund transfers.
Some unutilized funds require consultation with the donor agency to determine the appropriate process for refunding the same.
observations.reason: Difficulties are encountered in locating and retrieving the documents that serve as evidence of the fund transfer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management facilitate
the implementation of the development
projects within the targeted timeframe and
instruct:
(14) a) The Municipal Engineer to ensure that
detailed engineering investigations, feasibility
surveys, and designs, for the project, have
been sufficiently carried out and approved; and
contract plans and program of works
prepared before the preparation of the Annual
Investment Program; and
(15) b) The Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to periodically monitor the
implementation of the programs, projects, or
activities funded from the 20 percent Local
Development Fund.
observations.reason: The readiness and feasibility of the proposed priority PPAs under the 20% DF was not thoroughly evaluated prior to their inclusion in the AIP, resulting to delays in implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
(16) a) Instruct the LDRRM Office to review and
update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) to include the programs, projects,
and activities to be funded from the unexpended
balance of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2013-1; and
(17) b) Ensure that the LDRRMF, including the
unexpended balances of previous years,
is utilized only for programs, projects and
activities incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to avoid
irregularity in the disbursements of the same.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 did not include, under a separate caption, the activities to be funded from the unexpended balances of the prior year’s LDRRMF lodged under the special trust fund. Hence, the utilization of unspent DRRM funds totaling ₱6,924,399.00 was not incorporated into the planning process, as evidenced by their omission from the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the
personnel concerned to:
(18) a) Comply with the required public notices, as
well as its specifications and prohibitions, of
projects/programs/activities of the LGU, as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 (as
amended); and
(19) b) Inform the OIC-Audit Team Leader within
ten days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity
of any postings of project signboards and/or
public notice for validation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
This recommendation was applied only to NGA-funded projects. The Municipal Engineer endeavors to comply with this recommendation.
The auditor was not informed of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation.
observations.reason: The auditor was not informed of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct
the Goa Ecological Waste Management Office
(GEWAMO) to comply with the minimum
requirements for the operation of a controlled
dumpsite as provided in Section 39 of R.A. 9003
and Section 2, Rule XIII of its Implementing Rules
and Regulations, pending full operation of the
sanitary landfill, to ensure that adequate
environmental protection measures and
safeguards are in place for the closure and
rehabilitation of the said dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: Construction of the sanitary landfill and rehabilitation of the existing dumpsite are on-going.
observations.reason: Construction of the sanitary landfill and rehabilitation of the existing dumpsite are on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management institute
legal mechanisms to strictly implement the
prohibition on the use of non-environmentally
acceptable packaging and the banning of the use
of unnecessary single-use plastics in the local
government offices following Section 30 of
R.A. No. 9003 and NSWC Resolution No. 1363
series of 2020, to contribute to the national
government’s efforts of reducing waste generation
at source.
observations.managementAction: The proposed ordinance on single-use plastic is still under the second reading of the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management ensure
that all insurable assets of the LGU are insured
with the GSIS GIF so that the government
would be indemnified in case of theft, fire, or
any fortuitous events/casualties, as mandated
by Republic Act No. 656 (amended by
Presidential Decree No. 245).
(26) We likewise recommended that the
Management direct the OIC-GASS to prepare
the Consolidated Property Inventory Form
(PIF) and submit the same to the Audit Team
and the GSIS GIF, as outlined in COA Circular
No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: A Consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF) has been prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The appropriation for the insurance of the government properties is insufficient.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct
the concerned personnel to:
(27) a) Fully implement all the planned GAD
PPAs within the target implementation
period to ensure utilization of at least 5
percent of the annual budget to effectively
attain the LGU’s GAD objectives;
(28) b) Ensure that any changes in the GPB are
properly communicated to the DILG and
proper procedures are followed before
effecting these changes; and
(29) c) Prepare GAD AR following the procedures
indicated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 (as
amended).
(30) We likewise recommended that Management
establish and maintain a gender-responsive
monitoring and evaluation system to properly
assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on
gender mainstreaming.
(31) We recommended that Management
establish and maintain a gender-responsive
monitoring and evaluation system by creating a
Monitoring and Evaluation Team, and/or
instructing the GAD Focal Person to use
available monitoring and evaluation tools
indicated in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 (as
amended).
observations.managementAction:
During CY 2024, a revised GPB, incorporating the proposed changes to the previously endorsed GPB, was submitted to the DILG. As no action was taken by the DILG within 30 days of receipt, the revision was deemed approved.
GAD AR was prepared following the prescribed procedures.
The MPDO has developed a monitoring tool to track the implementation of GAD PPAs. Although, it lacks the capability to assess their impact.
A database and a monitoring tool were developed and maintained by the MPDC to facilitate proper planning and implementation of GAD PPAs.
No GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team has been created yet.
observations.reason:
Focal person was overwhelmed by the PPAs granted by national agencies, e.g., Supplemental Feeding, Social Pension, etc. which demanded full attention because of the deadline set to them.
As no formal endorsement was received from the DILG for the adjusted GPB, the LGU proceeded to implement the adjusted GPB and considered the supplemental GPB as received by DILG.
The MPDO has a monitoring tool but lacks capability to assess impact.
No Monitoring and Evaluation Team created yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management enjoin
the OIC-GASS, Municipal Accountant, and other
concerned department heads, to comply with
the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
Local Roads Asset Management System outlined
in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.reason: A variance still exists between the record of the GASS and the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:
(34) a) Accounting Office to record expenses
only when inventories are issued based on the
weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slip
(RIS) together with the Summary of Supplies
and Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted by the
GASS Office, observing proper use of account
titles guided by the rules provided in Section
4(s), Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs;
(35) b) GASS Office to coordinate with end-users
for the maintenance of stock cards in their
offices for inventories issued which should be
subjected to semestral physical count by the
Inventory Committee to facilitate verification of
the correctness of balances of the same and
ensure adequate controls and monitoring
thereof; and
(36) c) Inventory Committee to conduct properly
the physical count of inventories, including
semi-expendable properties, and the result
thereof properly recorded in its appropriate
reports.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of inventories and semi-expendable properties were conducted, and the corresponding RPCI and RPCSP were prepared.
observations.reason: There are difficulties encountered in determining the specific details of inventories listed in the SSMIs, particularly when no RIS is attached. Other offices responsible for the custody of supplies do not maintain stock cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
(37) a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update
the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s
index card based on the Assessment Roll and
furnish the Accountant a certified list of taxes
due and collectible at the beginning of each year
as the basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET
Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred
SET Income accounts; and
(38) b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record
RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of
each year based on the certified list of taxpayers
showing the amount of tax due and collectible for
the year.
observations.managementAction: The certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectible was belatedly submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The delayed submission of the certified list of taxpayers hindered the timely recording of RPT and SET Receivables at the start of each year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor,
in his capacity as the Chairman of the LDRRMC,
ensure proper and prompt implementation of
the PPAs embodied in the LDRRMFIP through
strict adherence to the pertinent provisions of
R.A. No. 10121.
observations.reason: Similar situation in CY 2024, where multiple PPAs with a total appropriation of ₱4,850,000.00 were not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GASS Office
coordinate with the Accounting Office once the
one-time cleansing of PPE is completed so that
proper reclassification of accounts related to
semi-expendable properties in the books is
effected.
observations.managementAction: Identification of obsolete and unserviceable properties was completed and awaiting action from the Disposal Committee.
observations.reason: Some accounts still need reconciliation and reclassification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the
Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to spearhead the setting up and
maintenance of the GAD database to serve as
the basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming, and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: A database and a monitoring tool were developed and maintained by the MPDC to facilitate proper planning and implementation of GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the
Municipal Accountant to analyze the affected
accounts to determine the cause of the huge
difference amounting to ₱6.27 million and
effect the necessary adjustments thereon if
warranted.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require
the GASS Office to institute measures to properly
dispose of all obsolete and unserviceable
properties pursuant to the directives of Section
79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160
and in accordance with the procedures cited in
COA Circular No. 89-296.
observations.managementAction: Identification of obsolete and unserviceable properties was completed and awaiting action from the Disposal Committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through
the MPMIU facilitate the turn-over of goods and
civil works to the Proponent Group through a
Deed of Donation and Certificate of Turn-Over.
observations.managementAction: Final Inspection Report and Subproject Turn-Over Certificate were submitted partially; Deed of Donation not yet enacted for the transfer of the facility.
observations.reason: As of February 28, 2024, only the Final Inspection Report and Subproject Turn-over Certificate pertaining to the Village-type Feedmill and Swine Housing Facility costing ₱8,885,488.67 were submitted, not to mention that no Deed of Donation was enacted yet for the transfer of said facility. Hence, only partial implementation was noted in the observed deficiency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the concerned personnel to:
(47) a) allot sufficient budget for the procurement
of regular supplies of Personal Protective
Equipment (PPEs) for the operations of Goa
Ecological Solid Waste Management Office
(GEWAMO);
(48) b) regularly provide complete sets of PPEs to
all collectors and other personnel directly
involved in solid waste collection, and perform
constant replacements thereof, particularly
when the same are damaged or in conditions
that are no longer safe to be used by the workers;
(49) c) establish institutional mechanisms to
educate and compel workers to comply with the
mandatory use of complete PPEs to protect their
health and safety; and
(50) d) provide trainings or coordinate with other
agencies such as the Department of Environment
and Natural Resources (DENR), Department of
Health (DOH) and Technical Education and Skills
Development Authority (TESDA) to conduct
trainings on proper handling of solid wastes;
observations.managementAction:
GEWAMO ensured the PPEs purchased would adequately support GEWAMO’s operations.
The LGU regularly provided complete sets of PPEs, including gloves, uniforms, boots, safety hardhats, and facemasks, to all relevant personnel.
There is lack of safety and health training; garbage collectors and other personnel are not accustomed to wearing the required PPEs provided to them.
This aspect necessitates closer and more stringent monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the GEWAMO to:
(51) a. conduct more extensive IECs in all
barangays to ensure wider dissemination of the
policy on mandatory segregation of solid waste
and the promotion of the use of proper garbage
bags or containers as storage of residual wastes
set out for collection;
(52) b. through the Eco-police Officers, strictly
enforce the “No Segregation, No Collection
Policy” in the daily waste collection process by
ensuring that the solid wastes are properly
segregated, and only residual wastes, i.e., non-
recyclable and special wastes, are collected; and
(53) c. monitor and supervise the regular duties of
Eco-police Officers and other persons charged
with the enforcement of SWM policies; conduct an
evaluation on the effectiveness of their
performance relative to the enforcement of the
mechanisms adopted by the Municipality to
ensure the proper segregation and collection of
solid wastes as designed in its Solid Waste
Management Plan; and, if necessary, provide
them with the necessary trainings to address
their performance deficiencies;
observations.managementAction:
GEWAMO has developed an IEC program called I-Easy-Goa (Information, Education and Solid Waste Management for You Goa).
This aspect necessitates closer and more stringent monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the GEWAMO to:
(54) a. evaluate the feasibility of extending solid
waste collection and transport services to the
upland barangays not currently covered by the
regular schedule, adjusting the frequency based
on the volume of waste generated (e.g. once or
twice a month), to mitigate the accumulation and
improper disposal of residual wastes by the
households. Alternatively, provide these barangays
with mechanisms to properly collect and transport
their residual wastes (e.g. provision of equipment
or vehicle);
(55) b. conduct regular monitoring to ensure that
barangays and households comply with the
provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 232 s.
2014, implementing the GEWAMP and imposing
penalties and sanctions for violations thereof; and
(56) c. intensify IECs on RA 9003 to ensure that
information on solid waste management and
related health and environmental concerns are
widely disseminated among the public;
observations.managementAction:
Since the implementation of GEWAMP through Municipal Ordinance No. 232, the number of barangays covered by the LGU’s solid waste collection service has gradually increased from 10 to 27 out of 34 over the past ten years.
This aspect necessitates closer and more stringent monitoring.
GEWAMO has developed an IEC program called I-Easy-Goa (Information, Education and Solid Waste Management for You Goa).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(57) a. through the assistance of GEWAMO and
General Administrative and Support Services
(GASS) Office, display the body number or plate
number and the contact number of LGU-Goa in the
body of all vehicles used in the collection and
transport of solid wastes, in compliance with
Section 24 of RA 9003; and
(58) b. ensure that the operation and maintenance of
vehicles used in the collection and transport of
solid wastes comply with required maintenance and
sanitary standards, in accordance with Sections 1
(d) and (e) of Rule X of the IRR of RA 9003 and the
IRR of Chapter XVIII of P.D. 856 or the Code on
Sanitation of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: For further verification. Some actions were taken to display identifying numbers; operation and maintenance compliance subject to further verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management facilitate the prompt implementation of
the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020.
We likewise recommended that Management require the:
a. GASS office to: (i) maintain the complete and updated details of the
RCPPE and Property/Equipment Cards (PECs) to support reconciliation
efforts; and (ii) promptly forward the complete documentation for all
donations in-kind received by the LGU to the Accounting Office for proper
recording in the books of accounts; and
b. Accounting Office to: (i) individually verify and validate the PPE items
ensuring that only those items that meet the capitalization threshold shall
be recorded as PPE, and refrain from aggregating semi-expendable
properties not purchased in bulk, to determine the correct classification of
the assets; (ii) review the depreciation/ lapsing schedule and ensure that all
PPE items undergo proper depreciation computation and recording; (iii)
coordinate with the Engineering Office to identify the completed projects
and facilitate the timely reclassification of items of Construction in
Progress accounts to their appropriate PPE Accounts; and (iv) prepare
and effect the adjusting journal entries to rectify the identified deficiencies.
Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:

a) instruct the GASS Office to maintain a Registry of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued (RegSPI) for each issued semi-expendable property in lieu
of Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC) using the format prescribed in
Annex A.4 of COA Circular No. 2022-004; and, based on the Inventory
Custodian Slips (ICS) issued to accountable officers, prepare a weekly
Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI) using the format
prescribed in Annex A.4 of COA Circular No. 2022-004, and submit the
same to the Accounting office;
b) direct the Accounting office to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record
the issued semi-expendable property as expense based on the weekly RSPI
submitted by the GASS office; and
c) order the GASS and Accounting offices to reconcile their records on a
periodic basis, so that discrepancies thereon may be immediately
corrected.
Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:

a. require the Municipal Accountant to:

i. strictly adhere with the prescribed guidelines under COA Circular No.
96-011 and the MNGAS for LGUs in the recording of financial
transactions by ensuring that only those included in the Report of
Checks Issued (RCI) that are supported with complete documentation
are recorded in the books; and

ii. draw the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the
reconciling items noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS);
and

b. instruct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to:

i. ensure that only those claimed checks are included in the monthly
Report of Checks Issued (RCI) by facilitating the immediate release of
prepared checks to payees; and

ii. closely monitor the status of checks issued to prevent the accumulation
of stale checks, as prescribed in the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System for Local Government Units.

Management was amenable to the audit recommendations. The penalties for the
returned checks amounting to ₱5,600.00 were already refunded by the person liable on
April 4, 2025, under Official Receipt (OR) No. 0217880.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entries to take up the PPE items not yet
recorded in the Trust Fund and to transfer the
completed infrastructure projects and
purchased assets to the General Fund, and
provide the corresponding depreciation
expenses computed from the date when the
assets were made available for use.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting journal entries have been recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct
the Municipal Accountant and other concerned
personnel to submit the lacking documents and
ensure that all future disbursements are
accompanied by complete documentation
before processing the claims therefor, as
outlined in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents for the advances, progress billings, and final payments on infrastructure projects were attached to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the
personnel concerned to ensure that the amount
of VAT reflected in the submitted bids for
infrastructure contracts conforms with the five
percent rate prescribed under DPWH
Department Order No. 197 s. 2016; and that the
quantity of materials indicated in the Program
of Work is the exact number in the Bill of
Quantities submitted by the bidders.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation has been complied with.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management continue
its compliance with the proper withholding of
contributions and loan payments from
employees’ compensation and ensure that the
same is remitted to the GSIS, HDMF, and
PhilHealth within the period prescribed by the
pertinent laws, rules, and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the recommendations.
Premium contributions belatedly remitted to the GSIS pertain only to salary differentials which normally take a few months before they are reflected in the eBilling and Collection System (eBCS) of the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct
the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly
observe the provisions of Item 4.1 of DBM Local
Budget Circular (LBC) No. 145 and Section
325(a) of R.A. No. 7160 by ensuring the
correct computation of the allowable
additional Personal Services (PS) budget and
closely monitor the expenses and obligations
to avoid excessive disbursements from the
PS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer strictly adhered to the guidelines on PS Limitations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant make a detailed analysis on the
RPT/SET Receivable accounts and their related
Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts, and
prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries pertaining to the unreconciled accounts,
if warranted.
observations.managementAction: RPT/SET Receivable accounts were adjusted and reconciled with the Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts as reported in the financial statements at December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management refrain
from hiring a permanent employee of the Local
Government Unit (LGU) of Goa concurrently
under a JO or CoS arrangement/agreement and
strictly adhere to the rules provided under
Section 95 of R.A. No. 7160 on the prohibition
against double compensation.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Transfer back the remaining balance of the Trust Fund-BAWASA 20% LDF or the General Fund-Proper as the case may be, (except fund needed for the completion of construction project under BUB-CY 2014) so that it may be used for other priority projects of the LGU; and

Refrain from erroneous charging against the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Will cause the transfer of the remaining balance ASAP.

No further transfer has been made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories; 

Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Acting General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials and maintain the prescribed records to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies; and 

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the semestral physical count of all supplies and materials of the LGU and prepare the reports thereon, copy furnished the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: No appropriation for the construction of stock room.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management execute all PPAs within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the Municipality’s socio- economic and environmental development projects.

We recommended that Management:

require the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects; and

subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the accounts and identified funds for liquidation/refund to donor agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;
create new positions and provide capable personnel who shall be in-charge with the issuance of Notice of Delinquencies (NOD) and the monitoring of the delinquent tax payers; and

intensify tax campaigns by sending treasury personnel to barangay especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.
observations.reason: Hindered by the occurrence of COVID-19 Pandemic that hampered planned activities from being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the immediate demand of payments from delinquent stallholders since these accounts have long been overdue.
observations.reason: Leniency in collection was implemented in accordance with the President’s directive in view of the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension;
observations.reason: Concerned officials were dismissed from service, although, two of them are now back in service. | These officials have existing leave credits that serves as guaranty until required documents are submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

     a) Require the GASS and Accounting Offices to comply with the required
        preliminary activities prior to inventory taking as part of the one-
        time  cleansing  of  PPE  accounts,  and  henceforth,  ensure  full
        implementation of the cleansing  procedures  prescribed  under  COA
        Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and


     b) Instruct the GASS  and  Accounting  Offices  to  exert  efforts  to
        reconcile their records on a regular basis  so  that  discrepancies
        thereon may be immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office  to  adopt
the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of supplies and  materials,
especially drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory  supplies,
such that purchases thereof shall be  recognized  as  asset,  and  issuances
thereof as expense upon submission by the GASS  Office  of  the  Summary  of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with  the  supporting  Requisition  and
Issue Slip (RIS).

      We likewise recommended that, for the  meantime  that  no  centralized
stockroom has been provided  for  all  supplies  and  materials,  Management
direct the GASS Office to coordinate with the RHU and  GMI  to  prepare  and
maintain stock cards for drugs, medicines, medical,  dental  and  laboratory
supplies which should be  subjected  to  semestral  physical  count  by  the
Inventory  Committee  to  facilitate  verification  of  the  correctness  of
balances of the same and ensure adequate controls and monitoring thereof.

We recommended that Management require the  Municipal  Accountant  and
Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular analysis, review  and  monitoring  of
all booked obligations to ensure that  all  current  accounts  payables  are
immediately settled, and only valid claims with supporting documents  remain
in the books.

      We  also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant   review   and
scrutinize details of the transactions  improperly  classified  as  Accounts
Payable,  and  if  justifications  permit,  reclassify  the  same  to  their
appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to include all properties in the RPCPPE and Property/Equipment Cards and see to it that the reports contain adequate details/ description, property number, remarks on the condition and whereabouts of PPE and other information necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation of records;


Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards in order to completely account for each item composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and

Order both the GASS and Accounting offices to exert all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon may be immediately corrected.
observations.managementAction:
Updating of PPE Ledger Cards is on-going.



Reconciliation of records is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the accounting office to observe extra efforts in facilitating accurate recording of transactions through maintenance of updated schedules/subsidiary records on CIP accounts to come up with reliable accounting reports.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents.

Record the appropriate adjusting journal entries to show the accurate balance of the cash in bank account after taking into consideration the necessary supporting documents and computations, if any, as bases in preparing the adjusting journal entries, in conformity with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and

Prepare the Bank Reconciliation promptly and on time, in conformity with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the rules and regulations on review of Appropriation Ordinances enjoined under the Local Government Code and instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from disbursing funds in excess of the allowed amounts for items of appropriation under the Sangguniang Panlalawigan Budget Review.
We recommended that Management instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to strictly observe the proper posting of advertisements in the PhilGEPS required under the RIRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the procurement procedures prescribed under the RIRR of RA 9184 and refrain from disbursements of the unexpended LDRRMF for disbursements not included in the Annual Investment Program.

We recommended that Management:

Undertake adequate planning and coordination among the offices concerned to ensure that correct/actual financial data are used as bases for allocating the funds to the intended PPAs;

Execute only those PPAs identified in Annual and Supplemental LDRRMFIP and AIP to avoid misapplication of funds to ineligible and unauthorized disbursements; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the form prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the pertinent Subsidiary Ledgers as required under Item 5.1.12 of the aforementioned circular, and ensure that utilization of LDRRMF is properly accounted in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as co-chairman of the Local School Board ensure the proper planning and utilization of programs/ projects/activities charged against the Special Education Fund and refrain from disbursing the fund for PPAs not allowed under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 (as addended).
observations.managementAction: Disbursement of such kind of expenditure was stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that if the LGU is compelled to abandon a current appropriation for a particular PPA and substitute the same with another one, the transfer of funds must follow the rules on budgetary realignment, budget augmentation and reversion of funds set forth in Sections 321, 322 and 336 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

facilitate appropriate action in the cancellation, replacement and adjustment in the books of accounts regarding long existing stale checks totaling ₱2,565,158.16; and

effect the necessary adjusting entries to record net reconciling items in the amount of ₱4,344,354.31 but ensure that the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers are supported with adequate documentation.
